MOU-2024-002 MediCare Hospital Clinic Basic Plan 15 Jan 2024 14 Jan 2025 ₹14,400 Under Review
MOU Preview — A4 Format
MPCC
BMW
MEDICAL POLLUTION CONTROL COMMITTEE
Biomedical Waste Management | Haridwar, Uttarakhand
Email: info@mpccharidwar.in | Web: mpccharidwar.in | GSTIN: 05AABCW1234A1Z5
Document No.
MOU-2024-001
MEMORANDUM OF UNDERSTANDING
(Under Bio-Medical Waste Management Rules, 2016 — as amended)

This Memorandum of Understanding ("MOU / Agreement") is entered into on this ___________, by and between the following parties:

First Party — Service Provider
Medical Pollution Control Committee (MPCC), a unit of Wizone IT Network India Pvt. Ltd., registered under the Companies Act 2013, having its principal office at Haridwar, Uttarakhand — 249401, authorized by Uttarakhand Pollution Control Board (UEPPCB) for collection, transportation, treatment and disposal of Bio-Medical Waste, hereinafter referred to as "MPCC" or "First Party".
Second Party — Healthcare Facility (Client)
___________________________, a healthcare establishment duly registered under applicable laws, generating Bio-Medical Waste in the course of its operations, located at Haridwar/Rishikesh region, Uttarakhand, hereinafter referred to as "CLIENT" or "Second Party".

WHEREAS, MPCC holds valid authorization from UEPPCB and CPCB for the management of Bio-Medical Waste; and WHEREAS, the CLIENT is obligated under the BMW Rules 2016 to ensure safe disposal of biomedical waste generated at its premises; NOW, THEREFORE, the parties agree as under:

1. SERVICE PLAN & SCOPE: MPCC shall provide BMW management services under the ___________ plan, including: collection of waste from premises, safe transportation in authorized vehicles, treatment/disposal at CBWTF, supply of colour-coded bags, containers and sharp boxes as per schedule, issuance of manifests, trip records and annual compliance certificate.
2. TERM & VALIDITY: This MOU shall be effective from ___________ and remain valid until ___________, unless terminated earlier per Clause 15. It shall be renewed by mutual written consent before expiry.
3. CONTRACT VALUE & PAYMENT: The total contract value agreed is ₹___________ (plus applicable GST @18%). Payment shall be made annually/quarterly in advance as agreed. Overdue invoices attract 18% p.a. interest. Non-payment beyond 60 days entitles MPCC to suspend services.
4. COLLECTION SCHEDULE: MPCC shall collect BMW from CLIENT's premises as per the route schedule allocated at the time of registration. The CLIENT shall ensure availability of waste in labelled, segregated containers at the designated collection point during collection hours.
5. OBLIGATIONS OF MPCC: (a) Maintain valid UEPPCB authorization throughout the term; (b) Provide trained, uniformed collection staff; (c) Provide approved vehicles with GPS tracking; (d) Issue manifests for each collection; (e) Dispose of waste at UEPPCB-authorized CBWTF; (f) Provide annual compliance certificate; (g) Maintain treatment records for 5 years.
6. OBLIGATIONS OF CLIENT: (a) Segregate BMW at source into colour-coded bags/containers; (b) Not mix BMW with general/solid waste; (c) Maintain waste generation log (Form I) as per BMW Rules 2016; (d) Ensure safe storage in closed containers until collection; (e) Not hand over waste to any unauthorized person; (f) Permit MPCC staff reasonable access; (g) Renew authorization from UEPPCB annually.
7. MATERIAL SUPPLY: MPCC shall supply initial stock of colour-coded bags, sharp boxes and containers as per Annexure II rates. Subsequent supplies shall be charged at prevailing rates. The CLIENT shall return puncture-proof containers for sterilization.
8. MANIFESTS & DOCUMENTATION: A signed manifest (Form II) shall be generated for every pickup. The CLIENT shall retain its copy for a minimum of 5 years. MPCC shall submit consolidated reports to UEPPCB as mandated.
9. COMPLIANCE CERTIFICATE: Upon completion of each year of valid service, MPCC shall issue a Certificate of Compliance (BMW Compliance Certificate) to the CLIENT, which may be submitted to UEPPCB/NABH/other regulatory bodies.
10. INDEMNIFICATION: The CLIENT shall indemnify and hold MPCC harmless against any penalties, fines or legal action arising due to improper segregation, illegal disposal or non-compliance with BMW Rules on the CLIENT's part. MPCC shall not be liable for consequences arising from CLIENT's failure to segregate waste.
11. FORCE MAJEURE: Neither party shall be held liable for failure to perform obligations due to circumstances beyond reasonable control, including natural disasters, floods, strikes, government orders, or pandemic conditions. The affected party shall notify the other within 48 hours.
12. CONFIDENTIALITY: Both parties shall treat all information shared under this MOU as confidential and shall not disclose to any third party without prior written consent, except where required by law.
13. AMENDMENT: This MOU may be amended only by mutual written consent signed by authorized representatives of both parties. No oral modification shall be binding.
14. ASSIGNMENT: Neither party may assign its rights or obligations under this MOU to a third party without prior written consent of the other party.
15. TERMINATION: Either party may terminate this MOU by giving 30 days' written notice. MPCC may terminate with immediate effect in case of: non-payment exceeding 60 days, illegal disposal by CLIENT, or CLIENT's licence/registration cancellation. No refund shall be made for the unexpired term in case of CLIENT-initiated termination.
16. DISPUTE RESOLUTION: Disputes shall first be resolved by mutual negotiation within 30 days. Failing resolution, the matter shall be referred to sole arbitration under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act 1996. Venue: Haridwar, Uttarakhand. Language: English/Hindi.
17. GOVERNING LAW & JURISDICTION: This MOU is governed by the laws of India, including the Environment Protection Act 1986, BMW Management Rules 2016, and applicable state laws of Uttarakhand. Exclusive jurisdiction: Courts of Haridwar.
18. ENTIRE AGREEMENT: This MOU, together with its Annexures, constitutes the entire agreement and supersedes all prior oral or written communications between the parties on this subject.
19. WAIVER: Failure by either party to enforce any provision shall not constitute a waiver of that right.
20. SEVERABILITY: If any provision is found invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall continue in full force.
21. NOTICES: All notices shall be in writing and sent by registered post, email (with acknowledgement) or hand delivery to the addresses on record. Email to info@mpccharidwar.in shall constitute valid notice to MPCC.
22. REGULATORY COMPLIANCE: Both parties shall comply with all applicable central and state regulations, including BMW Rules 2016, PCB directives, CPCB guidelines, and any amendments thereto from time to time.
23. INSPECTION & AUDIT: MPCC reserves the right to conduct periodic inspections of CLIENT's BMW storage area to ensure compliance. The CLIENT shall cooperate and provide access during business hours with prior notice.
24. DATA & REPORTING: The CLIENT authorizes MPCC to include its waste generation data in reports submitted to UEPPCB, CPCB, and the MPCC online portal (mpccharidwar.in). Data shall be used only for regulatory compliance purposes.
25. RATE REVISION: MPCC reserves the right to revise service charges at renewal, with minimum 30 days' prior notice. Continued use of service after revision date constitutes acceptance of revised rates.
26. LIQUID WASTE: This agreement does not cover liquid chemical waste, cytotoxic drugs, or radioactive waste unless specifically agreed in writing. Such waste shall be managed by the CLIENT independently.
27. CONTINGENCY COVERAGE: In the event of MPCC's temporary inability to collect waste (vehicle breakdown, etc.), MPCC shall arrange an alternate collection within 24 hours or notify the CLIENT to safely store waste until collection resumes.
28. COUNTERPARTS & DIGITAL EXECUTION: This MOU may be executed in counterparts, and a digitally signed copy shall be as valid as the original. A copy submitted through the MPCC portal (mpccharidwar.in) shall constitute the authenticated original for regulatory purposes.
Annexure II — Handling Material Supply Rates (Indicative)
Item Category Size/Capacity Rate (₹) per unit
Yellow BagCat 3/630L12.00
Red BagCat 430L12.00
Blue/White BagCat 5 (Glass)10L8.00
Sharp Box (Puncture-Proof)Cat 45L45.00
Sharp Box (Puncture-Proof)Cat 410L80.00
Yellow Container (Rigid)Cat 320L220.00
Black Bag (General)Non-BMW60L8.00
MPCC Biohazard Labels (Sheet)All20 labels20.00
* Rates are exclusive of GST. Bulk orders qualify for negotiated rates. Rates subject to revision annually.

🧰 In-Vehicle Compliance Kit

20-item weekly checklist · Photo verification · Per vehicle tracking

Mandatory 20-Item Compliance Kit (as per operational spec):
1. Emergency kit  ·  2. First-aid box  ·  3. Barcode scanner (handheld)  ·  4. Route chart / delivery manifest  ·  5. Weighing machine  ·  6. GPS device (active)  ·  7. Fuel sensor device  ·  8. Physical log book  ·  9. Driver ID card (with photo)  ·  10. Leak-proof vehicle body  ·  11. Biohazard symbol sticker (if applicable)  ·  12. Plant/depot address display  ·  13. Vaccinated driver/helper certificate  ·  14. Mask + apron (driver & helper)  ·  15. Vehicle papers folder (RC/Insurance/PUC)  ·  16. Spare tyre (inflated)  ·  17. Drum / container (where applicable)  ·  18. Paint / marking schedule (for specialised fleet)  ·  19. Fire extinguisher  ·  20. Reflective triangle / breakdown sign
2 Fully Compliant (20/20)
1 Partially Compliant
1 Non-Compliant
2 Photo Pending (weekly)

🔐 Roles & Permissions

Define access levels for each role — module-wise CRUD and export controls.

👑
Super Admin
Full access to all modules
2 users assigned
🛡
Admin
All except user & role management
3 users assigned
👔
Manager
View + approve/reject only
3 users assigned
🧾
Accountant
Finance & billing modules only
2 users assigned
⚙️
Operator
Data entry & operations only
3 users assigned
👁
Viewer
Read-only access, no exports
1 user assigned
Module-wise Permissions Matrix
Module 👑 Super Admin 🛡 Admin 👔 Manager 🧾 Accountant ⚙️ Operator 👁 Viewer
CRUDE CRUDE CRUDE CRUDE CRUDE CRUDE
✅ Allowed❌ DeniedC=Create   R=Read   U=Update   D=Delete   E=Export

🎓 App Tutorial & Help

Step-by-step guides for Admin, Driver App and Manager App

1️⃣
Register a Customer
Go to Customer Reg → Click + New Registration → Fill Institution Name, Type, Full Address, Contact Person, Mobile, Email, Aadhar No → Upload Aadhar + Reg Certificate → Select Plan → Pay 6-Month Advance → Submit.
2️⃣
Generate Invoice
Go to Invoice Management → Select Zone from filter → Click Generate Zone Invoice → For bulk: go to Bulk Invoice Generate → Select all parties → Click Generate. View invoice → Send via Email or WhatsApp.
3️⃣
Record Payment
Go to Bank Receipt Entry → Click Auto-Match UPI → Confirm matched payments → Click Post All to Ledger. For manual entry: enter amount, date, reference and click Post.
4️⃣
Vehicle Master
Go to Fleet → Vehicle Master → Click Add Vehicle → Enter Registration No → Select Zone first → Routes for that zone will appear → Select Route → Save. Zone options: UK, Kanpur, Gorakhpur, Jhansi.
5️⃣
Kit Assembly
Go to Inventory → Kit Master → You have 2 kits: Small Kit (for clinics) and Large Kit (for hospitals). Check available raw materials → Click Assemble → Enter quantity → Kits added to stock automatically.
6️⃣
Customer Login Issue
Customer portal: mpccharidwar.in/portal. If customer cannot login: (1) Check status is "Approved" in Customer Reg list. (2) Click Actions → Resend Credentials. (3) Verify mobile/email is correct. Credentials are sent on approval automatically.
📱
MPCC Driver App
Android app for BMW waste collection drivers
Android 8.0+
GPS Required
Camera Required
STEP 1 Login to Driver App
1. Download MPCC Driver App from link shared by admin
2. Open app → Enter your Driver Code (DRV-001 etc.) and Password
3. Allow GPS and Camera permissions
4. You will see your Today's Route on home screen
STEP 2 Start Collection
1. Tap Start Route — GPS tracking begins
2. Tap any party on the route list
3. Scan the QR Code at facility entrance
4. Enter waste quantity in bags/containers
5. Take photo of waste bags collected
6. Tap Confirm Collection
STEP 3 Mark No Collection
1. If facility is closed or waste not ready:
2. Tap the party → Tap No Collection
3. Select Reason: Closed / No Waste / Access Denied / Other
4. Take photo as proof
5. Confirm — Admin will see in No Collection Report
STEP 4 Fuel Entry
1. After refuelling → tap Fuel Entry in app
2. Enter: Litres, Rate per litre, Pump name
3. Select payment mode (Cash / UPI / Card)
4. Take photo of fuel receipt
5. Tap Save — entry goes to Fuel Log in admin
STEP 5 End of Day
1. After completing all collections → tap End Route
2. Review summary: Collected, Missed, Total KM
3. Tap Submit Day Report
4. Admin gets notification with day summary
5. GPS tracking stops automatically
TROUBLE? Common Issues
Login fails: Contact admin to reset password
GPS not working: Turn on Location in phone settings
QR not scanning: Ensure camera permission is allowed
Slow app: Check mobile data / WiFi connection
Can't see route: Admin must assign route to vehicle first
💼
MPCC Manager App
For supervisors and zone managers — monitor collections, approvals, reports
Android / iOS
Live Dashboard
📊 Live Collection Monitor
Home screen shows: Total parties today, Collected count, Pending count, No Collection count. Tap any driver to see their live location on map and collection status in real time.
🚐 Vehicle Tracking
Tap Fleet tab → See all vehicles on map with color codes: Green = On Route, Orange = At Party, Red = Stopped. Tap vehicle to see driver name, route, last update time, speed.
✅ Approvals
Tap Approvals → See pending: Customer Registrations, Purchase Requests, Advance Payments. Tap any item to Review and Approve or Reject with remarks. Approval sends instant notification to submitter.
📋 Reports
Tap Reports → Available: Daily Collection Summary, No Collection Report, Zone-wise Revenue, Fuel Expense. Select date range → View or Export PDF/Excel. Share directly via WhatsApp or Email from app.
💬 Messaging
Tap Messages → Send announcements to: All Drivers, Specific Zone, Specific Vehicle. Drivers receive push notification on their app. You can also send individual messages to driver.
🆘 Common Issues
Driver not visible on map: Driver app GPS may be off. Call driver to check GPS setting.
Collection not updating: Check driver app connectivity.
Approval not showing: Pull down to refresh.
Login issue: Contact IT admin for password reset.
🏥
Customer Portal
Web portal for registered customers — view invoices, payments, collection history
URL: mpccharidwar.in/portal
🔐 First Login
1. Go to mpccharidwar.in/portal
2. Enter mobile number registered at MPCC
3. OTP sent to mobile
4. Enter OTP → You are logged in
5. Set new password on first login
🧾 View Invoices
1. Login → Tap My Invoices
2. See all invoices with status
3. Tap any invoice to view/download PDF
4. Pay online via UPI / Net Banking directly
5. Receipt auto-generated on payment
📋 Collection History
1. Tap Collections tab
2. See all collection visits with date, time, qty
3. View photos taken by driver
4. Raise complaint if collection missed
5. Download CPCB compliance report

⚡ Trigger Type
📅
Due Date Based

Invoice Date Based

Overdue Escalation

Custom Schedule

📤 Notification Channels
💬
WhatsApp MessageSend auto-drafted message to HCF billing contact
📧
Email ReminderSend email with invoice details and payment link
📞
AI Agent Call LogRoute Manager receives call task with script
📱
SMSSend SMS to registered mobile number
🔺 Escalation Chain
No escalation — send once and stop
No response in 3 days → Assign Call to RM
7 days overdue → Branch Manager Alert
Sequential: WhatsApp → Email (3d) → Call (7d) → BM Alert (15d)
📝 Message Templates
💡 Use placeholders: {hcf_name}, {invoice_no}, {amount}, {due_date}, {overdue_days}, {rm_phone}

⚖️ 30 / 70 Salary Split

30% Attendance-based · 70% Work Output (Auto KPI score + Manager override) · Configurable per role

30%
Attendance Component
Calculated from monthly attendance %.
Full 30% if attendance ≥ 90%.
Proportional deduction below 90%.
70%
Work Output Component
Auto KPI score from system data.
Manager can override ±10% within band.
Drives performance-linked pay.
EmployeeRoleBasic Salary Att %30% Earned KPI ScoreManager OverrideFinal Score70% Earned Total PayStatus

Quarterly KRA tracker per role · Manager 1:1 log · Work Assessment = 70% salary input

Company Logo

Invoice Settings

Invoice numbering, due dates, penalties and automation

Show company logo on invoice
Logo appears in top-left of generated PDF invoices
Auto-generate invoice on 1st of each month
Automatically creates invoices for all active contract customers
Send invoice via WhatsApp on generation
Requires WhatsApp Business API to be configured
Send overdue reminder after due date passes
Automatic payment reminders on Day 1, Day 7 and Day 15 post due date

WhatsApp Business API

For invoice delivery, payment reminders and QR scan alerts

Connected
✓
WhatsApp Business API is connected via Meta Cloud API. Approved templates: Invoice, Payment Reminder, Visit Confirmation.
Send invoice via WhatsApp
Auto-send invoice PDF link on invoice generation
Send driver visit confirmation to customer
Customer gets WhatsApp message when driver scans QR at their site
Send payment due reminders

SMS Gateway (Textlocal / MSG91)

Fallback SMS notifications when WhatsApp is unavailable

Not Configured
⚠
SMS gateway is not configured. Configure it as a fallback when WhatsApp delivery fails for customers without WhatsApp.
